fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Continuity and effectiveness in topoi
Baby steps in higher domain theory › lifting and partial map classifiers

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Domains are usually defined as the algebras for a partial map classifier (pseudo-)monad, where \mathcal {O} is a dominance containing \bot.
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: L { \mathopen {} \left ( A \right ) \mathclose {}} : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( p: \mathcal {O} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( p: \mathcal {O} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( p: \mathcal {O} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( p: \mathcal {O} \right ) \mathclose {}} } } A^p
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The dominance \mathcal {O} is some kind of interval, as \bot \leq \top. Leoni Pugh and I are studying the partial map classifier for the simplicial interval \mathbb {I} in simplicial homotopy type theory.
